propositions: Implement an asymmetric SSHSIG attestation scheme that signs and verifies attestation bytes via OpenSSH ssh-keygen -Y using stdlib subprocess with no new core dependency.
sign() pipes the payload on stdin with no file operand and returns the armored SSHSIG bytes from stdout; verify writes signature and allowed_signers to temp files and feeds data on stdin.
verify invokes ssh-keygen via an argv array (never a shell), validates principal and namespace, and branches only on exit code returning certified on 0 and mismatch otherwise.
Verification must fail closed on tampered bytes, wrong namespace, unknown principal, substituted key, and expired validity window, while a sign/verify round-trip yields certified.
Validity windows are enforced via valid-before/valid-after in allowed_signers, requiring OpenSSH >= 8.9, which sets the version floor.
ensure_available() raises SshKeygenUnavailable when ssh-keygen is absent or below 8.9, and verify fails closed (never silently passes) when version detection returns None.
